description_long: This object consists of five surfboards, each with distinct color schemes and patterns, arranged upright and side by side. The surfboards feature streamlined, elongated shapes with pointed noses and rounded tails, typical of modern surfboard design. The visible surfaces display a variety of colors, including red, blue, white, yellow, green, and orange, with some boards featuring gradient or geometric patterns. The undersides of the boards reveal fin configurations, with each board having a single central fin attached near the tail. The boards appear to be constructed from a lightweight composite material, likely fiberglass over a foam core, with a smooth, glossy finish. No visible deck pads are present, but some boards have minimalistic logos or small graphic elements on their surfaces.
description: A group of five colorful surfboards with streamlined shapes, each featuring unique color patterns and a single fin, arranged upright. The boards have smooth, glossy surfaces and are made from lightweight composite materials.
description_view_0: Front view showing the five surfboards upright, highlighting their pointed noses, rounded tails, and distinct color patterns.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin profiles of the surfboards and the slight curvature of their bodies.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the undersides of the surfboards, including the single fin on each board and additional artwork or logos.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the slim edges of the surfboards and the arrangement of the fins at the tails.
attribute_brand_logo: One board (center, rear view) has a small, oval black and white logo near the upper third; another board (rightmost, rear view) has a small yellow symbol near the upper third. Other boards have no visible logos.
attribute_fin_configuration: Each surfboard has a single central fin (single fin setup), medium-sized, located near the tail and extending downward.
attribute_artwork_or_graphics: Several boards feature gradient color transitions, geometric patterns, and one has circular green patterns on the deck. Some boards have small logos or symbols, but no elaborate artwork.
